title: Keep your registration number
description: Ask to keep your vehicle’s registration number after the vehicle is off the road.
summary: Sometimes a vehicle goes off the road for a reason other than a sale – for example, it has been written off after an accident, it is being repaired, or it has changed from private to commercial use.

If you want to put the same number on a different vehicle later, you can ask the Barbados Licensing Authority to hold the registration number for you. The number stays reserved for up to one year after its expiry date.
You need to say why your vehicle is off the road. The form lists the three reasons that are usually accepted: total loss, under repair, or changed from private to commercial use.
If you have not used the number on another vehicle within one year, it goes back into the Licensing Authority’s pool and can be issued to someone else.

items: your full name, address, phone numbers and email
the registration number you want to keep
the reason: total loss, under repair, or changed from private to commercial

items.name: Proof of the reason
items.note: For example, an insurance write-off letter or a workshop estimate.

how_long: Approval is usually given within 5 working days.

options.heading: In person or by post
content: Take the completed Retention of Vehicle Registration Number letter to the Barbados Licensing Authority, or send it to The Pine, East–West Boulevard, St. Michael.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
BB11000

assumptions: ‘Approval is usually given within 5 working days’ is an estimate. The form does not state a processing time.
‘There is no fee’ is inferred from the form not mentioning one.
